summary: 9 product-like mentions surfaced from 26 qualifying HN stories.

items.name: PerryTS
items.summary: Native TypeScript compiler that emits native executables.
items.why_included: A Bun-thread commenter called it a very promising competitor to Bun.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote: "Regardless of Anthropic/ClaudeCode, PerryTS[1] looks like a very promising competitor to Bun."

items.name: Aube
items.summary: Fast Node.js package manager with a shared global store.
items.why_included: A Bun-thread commenter said it looked interesting and came from mise’s creator.
items.evidence: A commenter said: "Don't fret; the creator of mise has released a faster alternative: https://github.com/endevco/aube" and added that Aube "seems interesting to me".

items.name: Quint
items.source_story_title: Redis array: short story of a long development process
items.summary: Executable specification language for modeling and verification.
items.why_included: A Redis-thread commenter pointed to Quint as a formal-modeling option.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote: "Have you considered incorporating formal modelling? Like: ... [2] https://quint.sh/"

items.name: RandomX.js
items.source_story_title: How Monero's proof of work works
items.summary: JavaScript implementation of RandomX for CPU-mining experiments.
items.why_included: A Monero-thread commenter highlighted a JavaScript RandomX implementation for web-mining use cases.
items.evidence: A commenter said: "RandomX has some interesting use cases beyond Monero RandomX in Javascript (web mining?) https://github.com/l1mey112/randomx.js/"

items.name: Equi-X
items.source_story_title: How Monero's proof of work works
items.summary: CPU-friendly client puzzle used for Tor DoS protection.
items.why_included: A Monero-thread commenter noted Tor now uses an Equi-X derivative.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote: "PoW is useful in far more situations than PoS. A derivative of RandomX is now used to protect TOR too (Equi-X)."

items.name: TrellisWare TSM waveform
items.source_story_title: BYOMesh – New LoRa mesh radio offers 100x the bandwidth
items.summary: Scalable MANET waveform for large tactical radio networks.
items.why_included: A BYOMesh commenter said it would be a better fit for mesh networking.
items.evidence: A commenter said: "Something like Trellisware's TSM waveform would be a better fit: https://trellisware.wpengine.com/waveforms/tsm-waveform/ Nodes can cooperate to beamform and reach greater distances."

items.name: Radionor CRE-2 series
items.source_story_title: BYOMesh – New LoRa mesh radio offers 100x the bandwidth
items.why_included: A BYOMesh commenter suggested it as a comparable radio option.

items.name: JS8Call
items.source_story_title: BYOMesh – New LoRa mesh radio offers 100x the bandwidth
items.summary: Enhanced HF digital communication software for radio operators.
items.why_included: A BYOMesh commenter listed it among emergency-communication alternatives.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote: "For emergency communication? Iridium, zoleo, JS8Call, packet radio. Not LORA."

items.name: Social Animal
items.source_story_title: Talking to strangers at the gym
items.summary: Street-interview YouTube channel about talking to strangers.
items.why_included: A gym-thread commenter recommended it as a useful social-skills channel.
items.evidence: A commenter said: "The author would probably love this YT channel which is all about helping others come to the same realization as he did: https://www.youtube.com/@socialanimal"
